---
title: ""
---

::: {.section-card}

# About Me

I am a Statistics and Data Science student at UCLA interested in statistical modeling, data analysis, and quantitative research. My work focuses on using data to better understand real-world problems and communicate insights clearly through analysis and visualization.

Through coursework and projects, I have worked with methods including regression modeling, classification algorithms, ANOVA, and broader statistical learning techniques. I enjoy combining technical analysis with clear explanations so that complex findings can be communicated in a way that is both rigorous and accessible.

My interests span several areas including data science, applied statistics, quantitative finance, and research that uses data to study social and economic systems. I am particularly interested in how statistical methods can reveal patterns in large datasets and support evidence-based decision making.

Beyond coursework, I enjoy building projects that combine statistical methods with real datasets, whether through predictive modeling, exploratory data analysis, experimental design, or public-facing digital research.

:::

::: {.section-card}

## <i class="bi bi-stars"></i> What I Value

- Clear and thoughtful communication  
- Strong analytical foundations  
- Practical applications of statistics  
- Well-designed, user-friendly presentation  
- Using data to support better decisions  

:::
